sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, presents with a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She reports that the current stone has passed, with symptoms resolving within the past three days.
She denies any current pain or blood in her urine.
Past medical history includes hypertension and diabetes, for which she is currently on norvasc 2.5mg daily and metformin 500mg daily.
She reports having some difficulty adhering to a healthy diet due to her busy lifestyle, often consuming fast food and high-sodium foods.
Review of systems: The patient denies any chest pain, abdominal pain, or other symptoms.
Current medications: Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension; metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 150/90 mmHg.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs are clear bilaterally, with no crackles heard.
A grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur is present, as previously noted.
Abdominal exam reveals no tenderness to palpation or on the CVA.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, with the current stone having passed, but ongoing issues with hypertension and diabetes management.
Problem list: Kidney stones; hypertension; diabetes; insomnia.
Plan:
I prescribed metformin 500mg daily for diabetes.
I will give Angela a consult to a nutritionist to help with dietary changes to manage hypertension and diabetes.
I will also have her start taking her blood sugars before every meal to gauge her glucose levels.
For insomnia, I prescribed melatonin 10mg as needed.
I will refer Angela to Dr. Harris, a urologist, for further evaluation of her recurrent kidney stones.
Follow-up: Angela will follow up in three months to re-evaluate her blood pressure, blood sugar control, and kidney function.
